meta.title: Pricing
meta.description: Transparent pricing for L3 support, hotfix, and project engagements.

intro.label: Pricing
intro.heading: Transparent by design.
intro.sub: Three engagement models. No hidden fees. All prices are exclusive of VAT — EU reverse charge applies for cross-border B2B transactions.

categories.title: L3 Support for Legacy Systems
categories.model: Annual flat fee · per legacy system instance
categories.desc: Third-level escalation exclusively for legacy industrial software systems. Covers incident investigation, root cause analysis, workaround guidance, documentation, and remote consultation — across all covered legacy instances.
categories.excludes: Deployment to production systems, on-site visits, and new feature development are excluded and governed separately.
notes: Included hours are pooled across all instances within a tier — a quiet instance offsets a busy one.
Excess hours beyond the annual pool are billed at €100/hour with prior written approval.
Instances may be on different tiers within the same agreement.
Total pooled hours across all instances and tiers cannot exceed 2,500 hours per year (10 hours/day × 250 working days) — the maximum annual capacity of one engineer.
Business hours: Mon–Fri 09:00–18:00 CET, excluding Bulgarian and EU public holidays.

sla.definition: System fully offline, data loss risk
Significant data gap or integration failure

categories.title: Hotfix & Emergency of Legacy Systems
categories.desc: Analysis, preparation, and delivery of patches, configuration corrections, or emergency fixes. All artifacts are tested in-house before delivery. The client is responsible for independent validation in their own environment prior to any production deployment.
categories.excludes: Deployment to production systems remains entirely the client's responsibility. No liability is accepted for outages or data loss resulting from client-initiated deployments of delivered artifacts.
categories.outofhours: 1.5× multiplier — evenings after 18:00 CET, weekends, public holidays
categories.expenses: The hourly rate covers all standard expenses. Exceptional costs exceeding €1,000 (travel, third-party licences, specialised tooling) are borne by the client with prior written agreement.

categories.title: Project Work
categories.desc: Refactoring, new features, migrations, and integrations. Each project is governed by a Statement of Work agreed in writing before commencement, defining scope, deliverables, timeline, and acceptance criteria.

models.name: Fixed Price
models.desc: Applied when scope is clearly defined. Scope changes require a written Change Request.

models.name: Time & Materials
models.desc: Applied for refactoring or development work where the full scope cannot be determined in advance. Weekly timesheets provided for client approval.

categories.title: Exploration & Spikes
categories.desc: A Spike is a time-boxed investigation used to reduce technical uncertainty before committing to a full project. The outcome is a written finding — not production code. Common uses: feasibility assessment, architecture options analysis, protocol compatibility study, or vendor evaluation.
categories.excludes: A Spike does not produce deployable software. If the investigation leads to a project, a separate Statement of Work is required.
categories.expenses: All expenses incurred during the engagement (travel, third-party licences, specialised tooling) are borne by the client.

terms: L3 Support invoiced quarterly in advance.
Hotfix invoices issued upon delivery, payable within 60 days.
Project invoices follow the schedule in the relevant Statement of Work.
Late payments beyond 60 days accrue interest at 1% per month.
All intellectual property transfers to the client upon full payment.

cta.heading: Not sure which model fits?
cta.sub: Describe your situation and we will recommend the right engagement type.
cta.label: Get in touch
